featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 16 is a post-Origin round with seven games and three byes.
The Storm and Warriors are the cleanest reads because they combine home ground, spine stability and opponent disruption.
The Panthers are still a strong tip, but Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Brian To'o being managed on reserves keeps it off the charity-bet line.
Roosters versus Sharks is the toughest quality game because the Roosters regain stars while Cronulla get pack reinforcements.

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Do not force a charity bet while post-Origin backup decisions are live.
Melbourne's Maroons trio are expected to play but backup minutes still matter.
Penrith can still change complexion late if Cleary, Yeo or To'o are rested again.
Cronulla can turn the Roosters game if Addin Fonua-Blake and Briton Nikora back up cleanly.

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the positive Mark Hughes Foundation ledger at +$0.53.
Wait for final 24-hour teams before any stake discussion.
Treat Round 16 as a lineup-management round, especially in Titans v Panthers, Storm v Raiders and Roosters v Sharks.
Round 15 had no locked charity bet, so no ledger movement is recorded.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Storm at AAMI Park with Harry Grant, Cameron Munster and Xavier Coates expected to back up.
Warriors at One New Zealand Stadium against a Cowboys side still without Tom Dearden.
Dolphins form line against a Tigers side losing Kai Pearce-Paul long-term.
Tom Trbojevic returning for Manly against a rested Bulldogs side.

betCard.title: Round 16 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Friday 19 June to Sunday 21 June 2026
why: The ledger is positive at +$0.53 and this is a post-Origin round with several backup variables.
Storm H2H and Warriors H2H are the only price-watch candidates from the current read.
Neither becomes a charity bet without a fresh TAB price, stake, match, and explicit user confirmation.
risks: Origin backup and late-rest decisions can materially change the best plays.
Several favourites may be short enough that the value is gone before final teams.
Any new charity play needs recorded odds, stake, match, and final team context before kickoff.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
charity.nextDonation: After a meaningful positive bank is built
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.
Rounds 10 and 11: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 12: No charity bet locked; tips went 5 from 5, but the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 13: Penrith Panthers head to head at $1.62 won 20-18, returning $10.53 from a $6.50 stake.
Round 14: No charity bet was locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 15: No charity bet was locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
After Round 15: Ledger is 3-2, $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.

watchlist: Price-watch Storm over Raiders only if the backup status of Melbourne's Origin players stays positive.
Price-watch Warriors over Cowboys, but do not ignore North Queensland's forward return through Jeremiah Nanai.
Do not bet Panthers v Titans until Penrith's final team clarifies whether Cleary, Yeo and To'o play.
Do not use Roosters v Sharks as a charity bet without a clear late-mail edge.
Broncos, Eels and Rabbitohs are on the bye.
confidence: Storm over Raiders - strongest raw read at AAMI if Origin stars back up
Warriors over Cowboys - home and spine edge with Tom Dearden delayed
Panthers over Titans - class edge, Origin-rest caveat
Dolphins over Wests Tigers - form and injury edge
Bulldogs over Sea Eagles - rested Dogs, but Turbo return makes it live
Roosters over Sharks - home lean, Sharks pack danger
Knights over Dragons - home lean in the lowest-confidence game

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 16 starts with backup calls and no charity stake
lines: As of Thursday, 18 June 2026, Round 16 team lists and late mail have been checked against current published reports.
Round 16 has seven games: Knights v Dragons, Wests Tigers v Dolphins, Titans v Panthers, Bulldogs v Sea Eagles, Warriors v Cowboys, Storm v Raiders, and Roosters v Sharks.
The charity ledger remains 3-2 with +$0.53 settled profit because no Round 15 charity bet was locked.
Round 16 is live as a preview board only; no new charity bet is locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Wait for backup clarity before staking anything
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger is 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
Storm over Raiders is the cleanest raw tip, but only if Melbourne's Origin players back up as expected.
Warriors over Cowboys is the next price-watch candidate because Tom Dearden's delayed return leaves North Queensland short in the halves.
No Round 16 charity bet should be recorded without a fresh TAB price, stake and user confirmation.

sections.title: Round 16 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 16 fixture board
lines: Knights v Dragons - Friday 19 June, 8:00pm at McDonald Jones Stadium.
Wests Tigers v Dolphins - Saturday 20 June, 3:00pm at Campbelltown Sports Stadium.
Titans v Panthers - Saturday 20 June, 5:30pm at Cbus Super Stadium.
Bulldogs v Sea Eagles - Saturday 20 June, 7:30pm at Accor Stadium.
Warriors v Cowboys - Sunday 21 June, 2:00pm at One New Zealand Stadium.
Storm v Raiders - Sunday 21 June, 4:05pm at AAMI Park.
Roosters v Sharks - Sunday 21 June, 6:15pm at Allianz Stadium.
Broncos, Eels and Rabbitohs have the bye.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who gets upgraded or held back this week
lines: Upgrade: Storm at home, Warriors at the special One New Zealand Stadium fixture, Dolphins form, and Panthers class if their spine plays.
Hold: Bulldogs because they are rested but draw a Manly side with Tom Trbojevic named back.
Downgrade: Cowboys without Tom Dearden, Tigers without Kai Pearce-Paul, and Sharks if Nicho Hynes is held out another week.
Volatility bucket: Bulldogs v Sea Eagles, Panthers v Titans, and Roosters v Sharks.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 16 team-list details driving the card
lines: Tom Dearden's return has been delayed by a missed ankle fracture, keeping the Cowboys short against the Warriors.
Penrith list Cleary, Yeo and To'o on reserves again, so final-team confirmation matters against the Titans.
The Dolphins rest key Origin players but still keep enough form and structure to rate over Wests Tigers.
Manly name Tom Trbojevic back from a hamstring injury, raising the Bulldogs game's volatility.
Cronulla name Nicho Hynes and Blayke Brailey around the squad, but Hynes remains a watch and Toby Rudolf is out.
